description: Database schema and core data models for leads, campaigns, emails, and replies
steps: SQLite database initialises with all required tables
Lead model stores: business_name, owner_name, email, phone, website, town, source, score, status
Campaign model stores: name, status, template, target_criteria, created_at
Email model stores: lead_id, campaign_id, subject, body, provider, status, sent_at, opened_at, clicked_at
Reply model stores: email_id, raw_text, classification, classified_at
All models use Pydantic validation with proper types

description: Configuration management with .env loading and provider configs
steps: Settings class loads all env vars with defaults
Provider configs (Groq, OpenRouter, Gemini) load from env
SMTP config loads from env
Missing required vars raise clear errors at startup
Config is importable as a singleton

description: FastAPI application skeleton with health check and CORS
steps: FastAPI app starts on configured port
GET /health returns 200 with status and version
CORS configured for development
API versioning under /api/v1
OpenAPI docs available at /docs

description: Google Maps business scraper for UK local businesses
steps: Accepts search query and location (e.g. 'restaurants in Bristol')
Extracts: business_name, address, phone, website, rating, review_count
Handles pagination to scrape multiple pages of results
Rate limiting to avoid detection (configurable delay)
Saves scraped businesses to database as leads
Deduplicates by business name + postcode

description: Website crawler extracting emails, phones, social links, and tech stack
steps: Crawls a given URL up to configurable depth (default 3 pages)
Extracts emails via regex from page content
Extracts phone numbers (UK format)
Extracts social media links (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn)
Detects basic tech stack (WordPress, Wix, Squarespace, Shopify, custom)
Handles JS-rendered pages via Playwright
Respects robots.txt
Returns structured enrichment data

description: Email finder generating and validating email candidates from name + domain
steps: Generates email permutations from owner name + domain (e.g. john@, j.doe@, john.doe@)
Validates email syntax
Checks MX records exist for domain
SMTP verification where possible (catch-all detection)
Filters disposable email domains
Returns best candidate with confidence score

description: Lead scoring engine with rule-based and signal-based scoring
steps: Scores leads 0-100 based on configurable criteria
Website quality signals: has SSL, mobile-friendly, load speed, outdated design
Business signals: review count, rating, has website, has social media
Contact signals: email found, phone found, owner name found
Negative signals: no website, very low rating, closed business
Score stored on lead record, recalculated on enrichment updates

description: FreeAIEngine with multi-provider failover (Groq → OpenRouter → Gemini)
steps: Initialises with provider configs from settings
generate_email() produces personalised cold email under 80 words
Falls over to next provider when current hits daily limit
Falls over to next provider on API error
Tracks daily usage counts per provider
reset_daily_counts() clears all counters
Returns provider name and model used with each generation
Email output parsed into subject and body

description: Email template system with personalisation variables
steps: Template supports variables: {business_name}, {owner_name}, {town}, {pain_point}, {service}
AI generates unique email per lead, not just template fill
Pain points derived from website crawl data (no SSL, slow load, no mobile, outdated)
Subject line under 60 chars, mentions business or town
Body under 80 words, UK English, no corporate AI slop phrases
CTA: book a call OR reply YES

description: Async SMTP email sender with rate limiting and error handling
steps: Sends emails via configured SMTP server
Rate limiting: configurable emails per minute (default 2/min for cold outreach)
Handles SMTP errors gracefully (bounce, reject, timeout)
Tracks send status per email (pending, sent, failed, bounced)
Stores sent_at timestamp
Supports HTML and plain text
Includes required CAN-SPAM headers (unsubscribe link, physical address)

description: Domain authentication checker (SPF, DKIM, DMARC)
steps: Checks SPF record exists and is valid for sending domain
Checks DKIM record exists
Checks DMARC record exists and policy
Returns pass/fail per check with remediation advice
Warns if authentication is missing before first send

description: Follow-up sequence engine with configurable timing and conditions
steps: Defines follow-up sequences: delay_days, condition (no_reply/no_open), max_followups
Default sequence: 3 follow-ups at day 3, day 7, day 14
Generates unique follow-up content via AI (not just resend)
Skips follow-up if lead replied or unsubscribed
Scheduler checks for due follow-ups and queues them
Follow-up emails reference original email naturally

description: IMAP reply detection polling for incoming email replies
steps: Connects to IMAP inbox to check for replies
Matches replies to sent emails by In-Reply-To or subject line
Extracts reply body text (strips quoted content)
Triggers classification pipeline on new reply
Polls at configurable interval (default every 5 min)
Handles connection errors gracefully

description: Reply classification using free LLM (interested/not_interested/ooo/unsubscribe)
steps: Classifies replies into: INTERESTED, NOT_INTERESTED, OUT_OF_OFFICE, WRONG_PERSON, UNSUBSCRIBE
Uses FreeAIEngine.classify_reply() with provider failover
Classification stored on reply record
INTERESTED replies flagged for immediate human attention
UNSUBSCRIBE triggers automatic opt-out
Classification accuracy target: >90% on clear-cut replies

description: Lead pipeline with status tracking and activity log
steps: Lead statuses: new, enriched, scored, contacted, replied, interested, converted, lost, unsubscribed
Status transitions tracked with timestamps
Activity log per lead: scraped, enriched, emailed, followed_up, replied, classified
Pipeline view: count of leads in each status
Filter leads by status, score range, campaign, town

description: Campaign management with target criteria and batch operations
steps: Create campaign with name, target criteria (town, business type, min score)
Assign leads to campaign based on criteria
Campaign statuses: draft, active, paused, completed
Batch generate emails for all leads in campaign
Batch send with rate limiting
Campaign-level stats: sent, opened, replied, interested

description: Campaign analytics dashboard with key metrics
steps: Open rate tracking via tracking pixel
Click rate tracking via redirect links
Reply rate calculation
Conversion rate (interested / total sent)
Per-campaign breakdown of all metrics
Daily/weekly trend data
Best-performing subject lines and templates

description: A/B testing for subject lines and email body variants
steps: Create A/B test with 2 variants per campaign
Random split of leads into A and B groups
Track open rate and reply rate per variant
Statistical significance check before declaring winner
Auto-select winner after minimum sample size reached
Apply winning variant to remaining unsent leads

description: GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance engine
steps: Every email includes unsubscribe link
Every email includes physical business address
Unsubscribe link immediately opts out lead (one-click)
Unsubscribed leads never receive further emails
Suppression list checked before every send
Data retention: auto-delete lead data after configurable days (default 365)
Right to erasure: delete all data for a given email on request

description: Consent tracking and audit trail
steps: Log all consent events: opt-in source, opt-out timestamp, erasure requests
Audit trail searchable by email address
Export consent records as CSV for compliance review
Legitimate interest basis documented per lead source
Processing records maintained per GDPR Article 30

description: REST API endpoints for all CRUD operations
steps: POST /api/v1/leads — create lead
GET /api/v1/leads — list leads with filters and pagination
GET /api/v1/leads/{id} — get lead detail with activity log
PUT /api/v1/leads/{id} — update lead
DELETE /api/v1/leads/{id} — delete lead (soft delete)
POST /api/v1/campaigns — create campaign
GET /api/v1/campaigns — list campaigns
POST /api/v1/campaigns/{id}/generate — generate emails for campaign
POST /api/v1/campaigns/{id}/send — send campaign emails
GET /api/v1/campaigns/{id}/stats — campaign analytics

description: Scraping and enrichment API endpoints
steps: POST /api/v1/scrape — trigger Google Maps scrape with query + location
GET /api/v1/scrape/{job_id} — check scrape job status
POST /api/v1/enrich/{lead_id} — trigger website crawl + email finding for lead
POST /api/v1/enrich/batch — enrich all unenriched leads
POST /api/v1/score/{lead_id} — recalculate lead score
POST /api/v1/score/batch — score all unscored leads

description: Compliance API endpoints
steps: POST /api/v1/compliance/unsubscribe/{token} — one-click unsubscribe
POST /api/v1/compliance/erasure — right to erasure by email
GET /api/v1/compliance/audit/{email} — consent audit trail
GET /api/v1/compliance/suppression — suppression list export
GET /api/v1/compliance/domain-check — check SPF/DKIM/DMARC

description: Background scheduler for automated pipeline operations
steps: APScheduler runs follow-up checks every 5 minutes
Daily midnight job resets AI provider counters
Hourly IMAP poll for reply detection
Configurable batch enrichment schedule
Scheduler status visible via API
Graceful shutdown on app exit

description: End-to-end pipeline: scrape → enrich → score → email → follow-up → classify
steps: Full pipeline runs for a single lead from scrape to classification
Pipeline steps execute in correct order with data flowing between them
Failures at any step are logged and don't break the pipeline
Lead status updates at each pipeline stage
Activity log captures every pipeline action
Pipeline can be triggered per-lead or per-campaign

description: Docker containerisation with docker-compose
steps: Dockerfile builds the application
docker-compose.yml runs app with correct env vars
Container starts and passes health check
Database persists via volume mount
Logs accessible via docker logs
Configurable via environment variables

description: Comprehensive test suite with >80% coverage
steps: Unit tests for all core models and validation
Unit tests for AI engine with mocked providers
Unit tests for lead scoring logic
Integration tests for API endpoints
Integration tests for database operations
Test fixtures and factories for test data
Coverage report shows >80% on business logic

description: Complete project documentation
steps: README.md with project overview, quick start, and architecture
SETUP.md with detailed setup instructions
API documentation auto-generated from FastAPI
Architecture document in docs/architecture/current.md
All environment variables documented in .env.example
